mechanism_narrative: Lower and falling FEDFUNDS reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and increases risk-taking demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when policy remains near the lower bound.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle near the lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns following low or declining FEDFUNDS are not higher than baseline in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high UNRATE level signals weak household income growth and elevated default risk, causing investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 9.0 produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if mean triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in PAYEMS and RSAFS identifies a demand contraction in which fewer workers and weaker retail spending reinforce lower corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS weakness signal has no incremental explanatory power over either single series in out-of-sample tests, or if triggered 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving factory output and capacity utilization, which raises expected operating leverage and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean in walk-forward validation, or if the signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below recessionary levels signals reduced construction activity, weaker durable-goods demand, and tighter credit transmission, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 600 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or if triggered returns are not statistically below baseline at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of FEDFUNDS near zero and very high UNRATE identifies an aggressive policy backstop during severe labor stress, compressing discount rates enough to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25 while UNRATE remains above 8.0
failure_condition: Reject if the FEDFUNDS-below-0.25 and UNRATE-above-8.0 interaction does not outperform the single-series FEDFUNDS signal, or if average 60-day triggered returns are not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS pinned near zero raises inflation-risk premia and uncertainty about future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above its 12-month change while FEDFUNDS < 0.25
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS interaction is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or if it adds no out-of-sample likelihood improvement versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth signals broad real-demand expansion that supports aggregate revenue and profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with GDPC1 growth above zero do not deliver higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than quarters with GDPC1 growth at or below zero, or if the effect is unstable across two consecutive 5-year windows.
